text:
COMPREHENSIVE TUMOR/NORMAL PAIRED NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

INSTITUTION: Molecular Pathology and Genomic Medicine Laboratory
ACCREDITATION: CAP-accredited, CLIA-certified

PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
MRN: [REDACTED]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Attending Medical Oncologist, Medical Oncology]
INDICATION: Metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, stage IV (pT3b pN2 M1), with sarcomatoid differentiation (20%); molecular profiling to guide systemic therapy selection

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Tumor Specimen ID: [LAB-TUMOR-XXXX]
Tumor Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue block from open right radical nephrectomy (cytoreductive intent)
Tumor Site: Right kidney, primary tumor
Normal Specimen ID: [LAB-NORMAL-XXXX]
Normal Specimen Type: Peripheral blood (germline reference)
Estimated Tumor Cell Content (by pathology review): 65% (cleared from adjacent stroma and necrotic areas; representative section selected from viable, non-necrotic clear cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid component)
DNA Yield (Tumor): 1,840 ng
DNA Yield (Normal): 2,260 ng
Mean Tumor Coverage (on-target): 612x
Mean Normal Coverage (on-target): 438x
Panel: ComprehensiveOncology Panel v4.1 (505-gene tumor/normal paired assay; covers full coding sequences of all genes, selected intronic regions for splice-site detection, copy number analysis, MSI, and TMB calculation)
Sequencing Platform: Illumina NovaSeq 6000

QUALITY METRICS
All quality metrics met pre-specified acceptance thresholds. Sequencing uniformity, base quality, and adapter trimming QC passed. This report is considered clinically valid.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
CLINICAL SUMMARY OF FINDINGS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Comprehensive tumor/normal paired sequencing of this patient's right renal primary tumor identified multiple somatic alterations consistent with the genomic landscape of high-grade, sarcomatoid clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC). Key findings include biallelic inactivation of VHL (hallmark of ccRCC), loss-of-function mutations in BAP1, PBRM1, and SETD2 (chromatin-remodeling tumor suppressors residing on chromosome 3p), hemizygous truncating mutation in KDM5C, a subclonal TP53 gain-of-function mutation likely enriched in the sarcomatoid component, homozygous deletion of CDKN2A, and high-level amplification of MET. These findings collectively indicate a genomically complex tumor with multiple pathogenic chromatin-remodeling alterations, aggressive features (sarcomatoid dedifferentiation, TP53 mutation, CDKN2A loss), and a potentially actionable MET amplification. Tumor mutational burden is intermediate and the tumor is microsatellite stable. Germline analysis of the paired normal specimen revealed no pathogenic variants in hereditary renal cancer predisposition genes. See detailed findings below.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SOURCE ALTERATIONS: SOMATIC (TUMOR-ONLY VARIANTS CONFIRMED BY PAIRED NORMAL SUBTRACTION)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

SECTION 1: PATHOGENIC / LIKELY PATHOGENIC SOMATIC MUTATIONS

1. VHL вЂ” p.Arg200* (c.598C>T)
   Gene Function: von Hippel-Lindau tumor suppressor; ubiquitin E3 ligase complex component targeting HIF-1alpha and HIF-2alpha for proteasomal degradation
   Variant Classification: Nonsense (stop-gain); pathogenic
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 48%
   Zygosity (by copy number context): Hemizygous loss of function; second allele lost via chr3p arm-level deletion (see Copy Number Alterations, below)
   Functional Consequence: Predicted complete loss of function of VHL protein. VHL loss leads to constitutive activation of the HIF transcriptional program, driving upregulation of VEGF, PDGF, EPO, and other angiogenic/proliferative targets. This is the canonical molecular driver of clear cell RCC and is present in approximately 90% of ccRCC tumors.
   Clinical Significance: Pathogenic driver; biallelic inactivation confirmed. Supports histologic diagnosis of ccRCC. Consistent with diffuse membranous CA-IX immunoreactivity observed on IHC (CA-IX is a direct HIF transcriptional target). No direct targeted therapy available for VHL loss in approved agents, but HIF-2alpha inhibitors (e.g., belzutifan) represent an emerging therapeutic class in this genomic context.
   Germline Status: Not identified in paired normal (germline VHL analysis below).

2. BAP1 вЂ” p.Leu572fs (c.1712_1713delAA; frameshift deletion)
   Gene Function: BRCA1-associated protein 1; nuclear deubiquitinase; chromatin remodeler and tumor suppressor; involved in H2A deubiquitination and regulation of the Polycomb repressive complex
   Variant Classification: Frameshift deletion; pathogenic (predicted loss of function)
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 44%
   Zygosity: Hemizygous; second allele lost via chr3p arm-level deletion
   Functional Consequence: Frameshift results in premature termination at codon 573 (p.Leu572Valfs*2), eliminating the catalytic ubiquitin carboxy-terminal hydrolase (UCH) domain. Consistent with IHC showing BAP1 loss of nuclear expression in tumor cells (as noted in surgical pathology report).
   Clinical Significance: Pathogenic driver; biallelic inactivation confirmed. BAP1-mutant ccRCC is associated with higher tumor grade, sarcomatoid features, more aggressive clinical behavior, and poorer prognosis. Importantly, BAP1 loss has been associated with enhanced sensitivity to immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy (IO) in multiple retrospective analyses and subgroup data from CheckMate 214 and IMmotion151. BAP1 loss may also predict sensitivity to EZH2 inhibitors (investigational in RCC) due to synthetic lethality mechanisms.
   Germline Status: Not identified in paired normal.

3. PBRM1 вЂ” splice site c.1419+1G>A (intron 14 donor splice site)
   Gene Function: Polybromo-1; SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex subunit (PBAF complex); tumor suppressor
   Variant Classification: Canonical splice site mutation; pathogenic (predicted loss of function via aberrant splicing/exon skipping)
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 39%
   Zygosity: Hemizygous; second allele lost via chr3p arm-level deletion
   Functional Consequence: Disruption of the canonical GT splice donor at the intron 14 boundary is predicted to result in exon 14 skipping or intron 14 retention, leading to frameshift and premature truncation of PBRM1. In silico splice prediction tools (MaxEntScan, SpliceAI) assign high confidence (score > 0.90) to splicing disruption.
   Clinical Significance: PBRM1 is the second most commonly mutated gene in ccRCC (after VHL), with loss-of-function mutations in approximately 40-50% of tumors. Biallelic PBRM1 inactivation has been associated with differential responses to immune checkpoint inhibition versus VEGFR-TKI therapy; some data suggest PBRM1-mutant tumors may respond preferentially to IO, while other analyses show variable effects depending on co-mutation context. In this tumor, co-occurrence of PBRM1 loss with BAP1 loss is notable, as these are generally mutually exclusive events in the broader ccRCC population; co-occurrence suggests a more complex genomic subtype with potentially distinct biology.
   Germline Status: Not identified in paired normal.

4. SETD2 вЂ” p.Arg1625His (c.4874G>A)
   Gene Function: SET domain-containing protein 2; histone H3 lysine 36 trimethylase (H3K36me3); tumor suppressor; role in transcription fidelity and DNA mismatch repair
   Variant Classification: Missense; likely pathogenic
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 31%
   Zygosity: Hemizygous; second allele lost via chr3p arm-level deletion
   Functional Consequence: p.Arg1625His affects a highly conserved residue within the catalytic SET domain. This substitution has been reported in multiple independent RCC cohorts (ClinVar, COSMIC RCC entries) and is predicted deleterious by multiple computational algorithms (SIFT: Deleterious; PolyPhen-2: Probably Damaging; CADD score 29.4). SETD2 loss of function impairs H3K36 trimethylation, leading to defects in transcription-coupled mismatch repair and aberrant RNA splicing, as well as downstream effects on replication stress.
   Clinical Significance: Likely pathogenic driver. SETD2 loss-of-function mutations occur in approximately 15-20% of ccRCC. Loss of H3K36me3 may confer microsatellite instability-like repair defects at a local level; however, this tumor remains MSI-stable at the global MSI assessment scale. SETD2 loss co-occurring with VHL, BAP1, and PBRM1 loss on chr3p represents a pattern of broad chr3p tumor suppressor inactivation, consistent with the copy number data.
   Germline Status: Not identified in paired normal.

5. KDM5C вЂ” p.Gln690* (c.2068C>T)
   Gene Function: Lysine demethylase 5C (also known as JARID1C); histone H3 lysine 4 trimethylase; X-linked chromatin regulator and tumor suppressor
   Variant Classification: Nonsense (stop-gain); pathogenic (predicted loss of function)
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 52%
   Zygosity: Hemizygous (X-linked gene; patient is female; VAF of 52% consistent with hemizygous loss at the X locus; the remaining allele may be subject to X-inactivation)
   Functional Consequence: Premature stop codon at residue 690 results in truncation of the C-terminal PLU-1 domain, eliminating histone demethylase activity.
   Clinical Significance: KDM5C mutations occur in approximately 7-10% of ccRCC and have been reported as associated with the sarcomatoid/high-grade phenotype. As a chromatin modifier, KDM5C loss contributes to the overall epigenetic dysregulation characteristic of this tumor. Emerging data suggest KDM5C-mutant tumors may have increased sensitivity to mTOR pathway inhibitors and potentially to HDAC inhibitors, though this is investigational.
   Germline Status: Not identified in paired normal; consistent with somatic origin.

6. TP53 вЂ” p.Arg248Trp (c.742C>T)
   Gene Function: Tumor protein p53; master transcriptional regulator of DNA damage response, cell cycle arrest, and apoptosis
   Variant Classification: Missense; gain-of-function (GOF); oncogenic
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 22% (subclonal)
   Zygosity: Subclonal; heterozygous; no co-occurring deletion at 17p13 detected in this clone
   Functional Consequence: p.Arg248Trp is one of the most well-characterized gain-of-function TP53 mutations in human cancer. The arginine 248 codon directly contacts DNA in the major groove; tryptophan substitution abolishes sequence-specific DNA binding and confers dominant-negative activity over wild-type p53. GOF TP53 mutants have been shown to interact with transcription factors (NF-Y, SREBP, ETS2) to transcriptionally activate pro-oncogenic programs.
   Clinical Significance: Subclonal TP53 GOF mutation, VAF 22%, below the clonal threshold. The subclonal distribution and co-occurrence with the known sarcomatoid component (20% of tumor volume) raises the possibility that this mutation is enriched in, or potentially restricted to, the sarcomatoid subclone, consistent with published data showing TP53 mutations are significantly enriched in sarcomatoid and rhabdoid dedifferentiation in RCC. This may reflect clonal evolution and selection during tumor progression toward dedifferentiation. The presence of a subclonal TP53 GOF mutation is associated with adverse prognosis and resistance to some therapies. No homologous recombination deficiency signatures were identified in association with this variant.
   Germline Status: Not identified in paired normal; confirmed somatic.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SECTION 2: COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

1. Chromosome 3p Arm-Level Loss (chr3p21.31 вЂ“ chr3p14.2 encompassing the entire short arm)
   Type: Arm-level somatic deletion
   Estimated Copy Number: 1 copy (heterozygous deletion)
   Genes Affected (clinically relevant): VHL (3p25.3), BAP1 (3p21.1), PBRM1 (3p21.3), SETD2 (3p21.31)
   Significance: Chr3p deletion is the most common somatic copy number alteration in ccRCC, present in approximately 90% of cases. This deletion provides the requisite "second hit" for biallelic inactivation of VHL, BAP1, PBRM1, and SETD2, each of which harbors a somatic point mutation on the retained allele (as detailed above). Together, these alterations constitute a compound genomic inactivation of all four major 3p tumor suppressors.

2. CDKN2A вЂ” Homozygous Deletion (9p21.3)
   Type: Focal homozygous deletion
   Estimated Copy Number: 0 copies
   Genes Co-deleted: CDKN2B (p15) co-deleted; ARF locus (p14ARF, alternate reading frame of CDKN2A) also deleted
   Significance: Homozygous CDKN2A deletion results in complete absence of both p16INK4a (CDK4/6 inhibitor; RB pathway regulator) and p14ARF (MDM2 inhibitor; indirect p53 activator) protein expression. In the context of a subclonal TP53 GOF mutation, concurrent CDKN2A/ARF loss further impairs the p53/MDM2 and RB/E2F pathways, likely contributing to the high-grade and sarcomatoid phenotype. CDKN2A homozygous deletion in ccRCC is independently associated with inferior outcomes in multiple cohorts. May also confer sensitivity to CDK4/6 inhibitor combinations (investigational context).

3. MET вЂ” High-Level Amplification (7q31.2)
   Type: Focal high-level somatic amplification
   Estimated Copy Number: 7.2 (high-level gain, not meeting strict amplification threshold of в‰Ґ8 copies but considered high-level gain)
   Significance: MET (hepatocyte growth factor receptor; a receptor tyrosine kinase) amplification is an uncommon but clinically significant finding in RCC, occurring in approximately 2-5% of tumors. High-level MET copy number gain activates downstream signaling through the RAS/MAPK and PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathways, driving proliferation, invasion, and angiogenesis. Importantly, cabozantinib (XL184) is a multikinase inhibitor with potent activity against MET in addition to VEGFR2 and AXL, and preclinical and clinical data support enhanced efficacy of cabozantinib in MET-amplified/overexpressing RCC. This finding provides strong biologic rationale for consideration of cabozantinib as a preferred VEGFR/MET-targeted agent in this patient.

4. Chromosome 14q Loss (chr14q22.1 вЂ“ chr14q32.33)
   Type: Arm-level somatic deletion
   Estimated Copy Number: 1 copy (heterozygous)
   Genes Affected (clinically relevant): HIF1A (14q23.2) is hemizygous; PSEN1 and other loci affected
   Significance: Chr14q loss is among the most frequent copy number alterations in ccRCC and has been associated with adverse prognosis in several large cohort analyses. Hemizygous loss of HIF1A on 14q may modulate the relative contribution of HIF-1alpha versus HIF-2alpha to the hypoxia-inducible transcriptional program, as HIF-2alpha (encoded on 2p21, copy number neutral in this tumor) is not deleted.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SECTION 3: TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN AND MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 6.4 mutations/Mb
   Classification: INTERMEDIATE (TMB-Low: <6 mut/Mb; TMB-Intermediate: 6вЂ“10 mut/Mb; TMB-High: >10 mut/Mb per ESMO/SITC consensus thresholds)
   Method: Calculated across 1.1 Mb of panel coding sequence after filtering germline and known recurrent somatic artifacts. Somatic point mutations and small indels counted.
   Interpretation: The intermediate TMB in this tumor is consistent with the overall genomic profile. The SETD2 loss of function (impairing transcription-coupled mismatch repair at specific loci) and CDKN2A homozygous deletion may contribute to a modestly elevated mutational rate above the typical ccRCC baseline (median ~2-3 mut/Mb). TMB at this level does not reach the threshold typically considered predictive of robust response to immune checkpoint inhibition based on TMB alone (в‰Ґ10 mut/Mb). However, other biomarkers in this tumor (BAP1 loss, PD-L1 positivity) may be more informative for IO response prediction.

Microsatellite Instability (MSI): MSI-STABLE (MSS)
   Method: Fragment length analysis of 114 microsatellite loci across the panel; comparison of tumor versus normal
   Interpretation: No evidence of widespread microsatellite instability. The tumor does not qualify as MSI-High, which would predict response to pembrolizumab or other immune checkpoint inhibitors via the tumor-agnostic FDA approval. MSS is expected for the vast majority of ccRCC tumors.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SECTION 4: PD-L1 EXPRESSION (REPORTED FROM COMPANION PATHOLOGY)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

PD-L1 (28-8 PharmDx assay): Tumor Proportion Score (TPS) 8%
   Note: The 22C3 PharmDx assay (Combined Positive Score, CPS) was separately reported on the surgical pathology report as CPS 14. These two metrics are not directly interchangeable; the CPS accounts for both tumor and immune cell staining.
   Interpretation: PD-L1 TPS of 8% and CPS of 14% are each consistent with PD-L1-positive disease by common thresholds used in RCC clinical trials (в‰Ґ1% TPS or в‰Ґ1% CPS). PD-L1 positivity in this context further supports the potential benefit of immune checkpoint inhibitor-based therapy (e.g., ipilimumab + nivolumab), in addition to the BAP1 loss and sarcomatoid histology.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SECTION 5: MUTATIONAL SIGNATURE ANALYSIS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Dominant Mutational Signatures Identified (COSMIC v3.3 framework):
   - SBS1 (Age-related spontaneous deamination of 5-methylcytosine): Present; consistent with patient age
   - SBS5 (Clock-like signature, transcriptional strand-biased, ubiquitous): Present
   - SBS40 (Unknown etiology; frequently observed in renal cell carcinoma): Present; dominant signature in this tumor
   - SBS8 (Possible association with double-strand break repair defects): Minor component
   No tobacco-related signatures (SBS4, SBS29) detected, consistent with reported nonsmoker status.
   No APOBEC signatures (SBS2/SBS13) detected.
   No platinum-related or alkylating agent signatures detected.
   Interpretation: The mutational signature profile is consistent with sporadic, age-associated ccRCC without evidence of exogenous carcinogen exposure.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

HRD Score: 18 (HRD-Low; threshold for HRD-High: в‰Ґ42 by academic scoring methodology)
BRCA1: No mutation detected (somatic or germline)
BRCA2: No mutation detected (somatic or germline)
HRD-associated genes (ATM, CDK12, PALB2, RAD51C, RAD51D, CHEK2, BRIP1): No pathogenic alterations detected in tumor or germline
Interpretation: No evidence of homologous recombination deficiency. This tumor is not predicted to be sensitive to PARP inhibitors or platinum-based agents on the basis of HRD.

SECTION 8: GERMLINE ANALYSIS (PAIRED NORMAL вЂ“ PERIPHERAL BLOOD)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

Germline analysis was performed on the paired peripheral blood specimen as the constitutional reference. The following hereditary renal cancer predisposition genes were specifically assessed:

VHL: No germline pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant detected
BAP1: No germline pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant detected
FH (fumarate hydratase): No germline pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant detected
SDHA: No germline pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant detected
SDHB: No germline pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant detected
SDHC: No germline pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant detected
SDHD: No germline pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant detected
MET: No germline pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant detected (note: somatic MET amplification identified as described above; germline MET mutations causing hereditary papillary RCC type 1 are distinct events and not identified here)
PBRM1: No germline variant detected
BRCA1/BRCA2: No germline pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant detected
TP53 (Li-Fraumeni): No germline variant detected
CDKN2A: No germline variant detected

Germline Interpretation: No pathogenic germline variants identified in hereditary renal cancer susceptibility genes. The patient's family history of renal cancer (maternal aunt, 70s) does not appear to be explained by an identifiable germline predisposition in the genes analyzed. This result does not exclude the possibility of variants in genes not captured by this panel, large structural rearrangements, or promoter methylation events. Formal genetic counseling referral is recommended per standard of care to discuss these findings and residual risk assessment.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
SECTION 10: INTERPRETIVE COMMENT
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

This tumor harbors a genomic profile fully consistent with high-grade, aggressive clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid dedifferentiation. The co-occurrence of biallelic VHL, BAP1, PBRM1, and SETD2 inactivation вЂ” all driven by the chromosome 3p arm-level deletion providing the shared second hit вЂ” represents one of the most genomically complex chromatin-remodeling alteration landscapes observed in ccRCC. BAP1 and PBRM1 co-mutation, while rare (each typically mutually exclusive in the broader population), has been described in a small subset of aggressive tumors and may define a distinct high-risk molecular subtype.

Of particular clinical relevance:

(1) MET amplification (copy number 7.2) provides a compelling biologic rationale for preferential use of cabozantinib over other VEGFR-targeted agents (sunitinib, pazopanib, axitinib) in this patient, given cabozantinib's documented MET inhibitory activity. Retrospective analyses from the CABOSUN and METEOR trials have suggested MET-high tumors may derive differential benefit from cabozantinib.

(2) BAP1 loss, PD-L1 positivity (CPS 14 / TPS 8%), sarcomatoid differentiation (20%), and intermediate IMDC risk collectively support the use of IO-based combination first-line therapy (ipilimumab + nivolumab per CheckMate 214), with BAP1 loss being one of the most consistently reported molecular predictors of IO over TKI benefit in ccRCC.

(3) The subclonal TP53 p.Arg248Trp GOF mutation (VAF 22%) is likely enriched in the sarcomatoid subclone. Sarcomatoid dedifferentiation is independently associated with worse prognosis and may predict differential response to IO over TKI. The presence of this subclonal oncogenic driver underscores the biological aggressiveness of this tumor.

(4) CDKN2A homozygous deletion is an additional adverse prognostic marker and may contribute to resistance mechanisms. In the context of this overall profile, clinical trial enrollment should be considered at disease progression, particularly trials evaluating HIF-2alpha inhibitors, MET-directed therapies, CDK4/6 inhibitor combinations, or EZH2 inhibitors in the setting of BAP1/PBRM1/SETD2 co-mutation.

(5) The intermediate TMB (6.4 mut/Mb) and MSS status do not provide independent support for IO use but are not contraindications.

(6) No germline pathogenic variants identified. Genetic counseling referral recommended.
